Title: MaRAP2-4, a waterlogging-responsive ERF from Mentha, regulates bidirectional sugar transporter AtSWEET10 to modulate stress response in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Up-regulation of AtSWEET10 is associated with increased soluble sugar accumulation and enhanced tolerance to waterlogging, and likely contributes to drought and salt tolerance in MaRAP2-4-overexpressing Arabidopsis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter EMSA; yeast one-hybrid; qRT-PCR expression analysis in transgenic lines; soluble sugar quantification; microarray profiling
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Waterlogging/hypoxia; also assessed in drought (mannitol) and salt stress contexts through MaRAP2-4 transgenic plants
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in MaRAP2-4-overexpressing Arabidopsis under both control and waterlogging conditions; highest fold induction among sugar-responsive genes in microarray
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AtSWEET10 promoter contains 3 DRE and 1 GCC motifs; MaRAP2-4 bound two promoter positions, one with overlapping DRE/GCC and one with DRE only. The paper also notes a possible GA uptake role reported elsewhere, but this was not tested here.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtSWEET10 was the most strongly induced sugar transport-related gene in MaRAP2-4 transgenic Arabidopsis under the analyzed conditions. 2. Its promoter contains DRE/GCC motifs that are directly bound by MaRAP2-4 in EMSA and Y1H assays. 3. Increased AtSWEET10 expression correlates with higher soluble sugar content and improved waterlogging tolerance, indicating a role in stress-associated sugar redistribution.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Whole plant tissues; required sink/target tissues under stress
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Carbon and energy supply, source–sink transport, osmotic adjustment, and support of abiotic stress tolerance under hypoxia/waterlogging, drought, and salt stress

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MaRAP2-4 positively regulates soluble sugar accumulation and enhances waterlogging tolerance, while also improving drought and salt tolerance in transgenic Arabidopsis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous overexpression in Arabidopsis; EMSA to cis-elements and AtSWEET10 promoter; yeast one-hybrid; transactivation assay and domain mapping; subcellular localization; qRT-PCR; microarray; physiological, antioxidant, and soluble sugar measurements
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-responsive positive regulator that directly activates AtSWEET10 and likely broader sugar metabolism/transport programs, making it a key upstream switch connecting stress signaling to carbohydrate availability under waterlogging.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Waterlogging; ethylene; methyl jasmonate; ABA; drought; salt; cold; mannitol treatment
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed in transgenic Arabidopsis under 35S promoter; higher transgene expression in HE lines correlates with stronger tolerant phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In Mentha arvensis, strongly induced by waterlogging and ethylene; also induced by drought, salt, cold, ABA, and MeJA
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Sugar transport and carbohydrate metabolism/availability under stress; indirectly antioxidant/redox homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MaRAP2-4 is a group I (A-6) ERF closely related to RAP2-4/ERF60, not a group VII hypoxia ERF, and lacks the N-end rule Met-Cys motif. It binds multiple cis-elements (DRE, JARE, GCC), indicating broad regulatory capacity. A 20-aa transactivation region (PLPSSVDAKLEAICQSLAIN) containing a predicted 9-aa TAD was mapped.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: AtSWEET10; broader set of carbohydrate metabolism/transport genes with DRE/JARE/GCC motifs, including SWEET1, STP5, STP14, ERD6, ADH, SUS1, SUS3, PPDK1, GAD3, CWINV4, TPPB, PDC1
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2/ERF family transcription factor; group I (A-6) ERF
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation via direct binding to DRE, JARE, and GCC cis-elements
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates AtSWEET10 directly; up-regulates multiple sugar metabolism/transport and antioxidant-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MaRAP2-4 is a waterlogging-, ethylene-, and MeJA-responsive ERF from Mentha arvensis that functions as a nuclear transcriptional activator. 2. Overexpression in Arabidopsis increases soluble sugar content, improves antioxidant capacity, and enhances tolerance to waterlogging, drought, and salt stress. 3. MaRAP2-4 directly binds DRE/GCC-containing regions of the AtSWEET10 promoter, linking stress signaling to sugar transport and carbohydrate availability.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Whole plant tissues; required tissues/sinks under stress
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provide carbon and energy during hypoxia and other abiotic stresses, contribute to osmotic adjustment and signaling, and support stress recovery and tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Waterlogging/hypoxia-associated cues; ethylene; methyl jasmonate; ABA; drought; salt; cold
